About

Engineering Across
Two Disciplines

I'm Joel Cardoza — a Mechanical and Biomedical Design Engineer with an MSc from the University of Dundee, UK (Merit, 2:1). My background bridges precision mechanical engineering and cutting-edge medical device development.

From designing soft robotic colonoscopy devices at Ninewells Hospital NHS to 3D printing human organ models from CT data for my thesis, I bring a rare combination of clinical rigour and real-world industrial experience.

I work best at the intersection of complex design challenges, simulation-led validation, and cross-functional clinical collaboration — translating needs into products that are safe, manufacturable, and compliant.

Currently based in India, actively seeking Design, R&D, or CAE engineering roles at multinationals in Bangalore, Chennai, or Hyderabad.

Portfolio

Featured Projects

Original engineering work spanning surgical instruments, orthopaedic implants, biomedical 3D printing, and NHS medical device R&D.

Surgical Instrument · SolidWorks · Multi-part Assembly
Femoral Impactor — Full Assembly
Original Design

A fully designed femoral impactor surgical instrument used in Total Hip Replacement surgery to seat the femoral stem into the medullary canal. Multi-part assembly: skeletonised handle, ergonomic curved retaining arms, tapered impactor body, and rubber-tipped head for controlled impact force transmission. Demonstrates advanced SolidWorks surfacing, DFM principles, and professional rendering.

SolidWorksMulti-part AssemblySurgical InstrumentTHRDFM316L Stainless
// click to switch view
Clinical context Femoral impactors drive the prosthetic stem into the medullary canal during THR. Precision geometry is critical to correct seating.
Key features Skeletonised handle · Curved retaining arms · Rubber impact tip · Full exploded view
Orthopaedic Implant · Autodesk Inventor · Full Assembly
Total Hip Replacement — 4-Part Implant System
Original Design

Complete THR implant system designed from scratch in Autodesk Inventor. All 4 components individually modelled and fully assembled: femoral stem (Ti-6Al-4V, CCD angle 127°), femoral head (CoCrMo, morse taper bore), UHMWPE polyethylene liner (snap-fit locking rim), and acetabular shell (porous Ti coating, screw fixation holes). Standards: ISO 5832 · ISO 21534 · ASTM F2033.

Autodesk Inventor4-Part AssemblyTHRTi-6Al-4VCoCrMoUHMWPEISO 5832
// click to switch view
4 components Stem · Head · Acetabular Shell · UHMWPE Liner — fully constrained assembly
Standards ISO 5832 (materials) · ISO 21534 (joint replacements) · ASTM F2033
MSc Thesis · University of Dundee · Simpleware · Meshmixer · PrusaSlicer
3D Printing of Human Organs — Heart, Kidney & Spleen
MSc Thesis · Merit 2023

Full MSc Biomedical Engineering thesis on 3D printing non-biological human organ models for surgical training. Complete pipeline: CT DICOM import → Simpleware ScanIP multi-region segmentation → STL export → Meshmixer mesh repair, stability and overhang analysis → print orientation optimisation → PrusaSlicer/Makerbot slicing → FDM output. Three organs: Heart (342k triangles, 5h 3m), Kidney (2 halves, renal vasculature, 4h 34m), Spleen (52,402mm³).

Design Engineer · Ninewells Hospital NHS · Dr. Luigi Manfredi · SolidWorks
Soft Robotic Colonoscopy Device — R&D Work
Professional Work
🔒 NDA — Selected Views

Design engineering role at Ninewells Hospital NHS Scotland under Dr. Luigi Manfredi. Contributed to the mechanical design and development of a soft robotic colonoscopy device. Responsibilities included SolidWorks CAD modelling of components and assemblies, design iteration from clinical feedback, additive manufacturing (FDM, SLA, SLS) for rapid prototyping, and ISO 13485-compliant design documentation. Specific details are subject to NDA.
